title: Spira Project Structure
description: Defines the project structure to be provisioned in a Spira instance via the REST API. Pass this schema to an LLM to generate a valid structure file from a plain-English brief.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor support.

program.description: The top-level Spira program (portfolio) that groups all products. The program must already exist in the Spira admin UI - it cannot be created via the API.

name.description: The exact name of an existing program in Spira.

products.description: One or more products (projects) to create within the program.

product.description: A Spira product (project) to create within the program.

name.description: Display name for the product. Must be unique within the program.

releases.description: Releases (test periods / sprints) to create within this product.

customFields.description: Custom fields to add to test cases and test sets within this product. Supports list, multilist, text, integer, decimal, boolean, date, datetime, user, and release types.

testCases.description: Custom list fields to add to test case artifacts.

testSets.description: Custom list fields to add to test set artifacts.

testFolders.description: Test case folders to create within this product, each optionally containing test cases.

requirements.description: Requirements to create within this product, supporting hierarchical nesting.

risks.description: Risks to create within this product.

tasks.description: Tasks to create within this product.

associations.description: Associations (links) between artifacts to create within this product. Processed after all other artifact types.

release.description: A release or test period within a product. Created as Sprint type by default.

customField.description: A custom field to add to a Spira artifact type. The 'type' determines what kind of input appears on the artifact form. List and multilist types require a 'values' array; all other types are standalone.

name.description: Display name for the custom field as it will appear in the Spira UI.

type.description:
The field type. list and multilist require a 'values' array. All other types are standalone and need no additional configuration.
  list      — single-select dropdown
  multilist — multi-select dropdown
  text      — free-text string
  integer   — whole number
  decimal   — floating-point number
  boolean   — yes/no toggle
  date      — date picker
  datetime  — date and time picker
  user      — Spira user picker
  release   — Spira release picker

values.description: Required for type 'list' and 'multilist'. The allowed dropdown values.

testFolder.description: A folder for organising test cases within a product.

name.description: Display name for the test folder.

testCases.description: Test cases to create inside this folder.

testCase.description: A single test case to create within a test folder.

name.description: Display name for the test case. Should clearly describe what is being tested.

description.description: A brief description of what the test case verifies and how.
examples: Verify that a standard sales order can be created successfully using transaction VA01.

requirement.description: A requirement to create within a product. Supports arbitrary nesting via the children array to represent Spira's hierarchical requirement structure.

children.description: Child requirements nested under this requirement.

description.description: A brief description of the risk.

probability.description: Likelihood of the risk occurring (1-5 scale).

impact.description: Severity if the risk occurs (1-5 scale).

task.description: A task to create within a product.

description.description: A brief description of the task.

taskType.description: The task type name (resolved to TaskTypeId at runtime).

association.description: A directional link between two Spira artifacts. The source and dest fields reference artifacts by their display name, which are resolved to numeric IDs at runtime using the provisioning summary.

sourceType.description: The artifact type of the source artifact.

source.description: The display name of the source artifact to link from.

destType.description: The artifact type of the destination artifact.

dest.description: The display name of the destination artifact to link to.

comment.description: An optional free-text description of the association.
